title: The Confession of Peter
subtitle: At Caesarea Philippi, the Lord asks: "Who do you say that I am?"

stillness: He was alone, praying, upon the way.
Come apart from the crowd and its opinions.
Be still, and let the truth be told to you — not by the multitude, but by revelation.
gospel: Jesus came into the regions of Caesarea Philippi.
And while He was alone, praying upon the way, He questioned His disciples,
saying: Whom do men say that I, the Son of Man, am?
And they answered: Some say John the Baptist;
others, Elijah; others, Jeremiah, or one of the prophets.
Jesus said to them: But you — whom do you say that I am?
Peter, answering for them all, said:
You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.
behold: After all that went before, Jesus comes as Saviour,
everywhere scattering the light of His doctrine,
He comes to the city called Caesarea Philippi —
set at the foot of Mount Lebanon,
where from the sloping side of the mountain two springs rise, called Jor and Dan,
which, flowing together at the root of Lebanon, make the Jordan.
Here, in the land of the Gentiles, Christ willed to reveal the mystery of His Incarnation,
showing that the foundation of the Church is laid in the faith of the Gentiles.
In the very place where the world paid its tribute to Caesar,
the King of kings and Lord of the world demands the tribute of faith —
a tribute paid from the head of the soul, that is, from the mind.
See Him: alone, praying upon the way, He turns and questions His own.
consider: He asks, not because He doubted or was ignorant, nor that He might learn something,
but to instruct them in the truth and to teach them,
and to wipe away the error of men through the confession of His disciples.
Before He tests the faith of His own, He asks first what the crowd says;
for, meaning to confirm them in the faith, He first willed to remove the opinions and errors of others from their minds.
So must the teacher of the Gospel first destroy errors, that he may the better build up the truth.
And the opinions of men were many.
Some said John the Baptist, raised from the dead — so Herod believed.
Others, Elijah, for the zeal of truth he had, and because he called down fire from heaven.
Others, Jeremiah, for his holiness, and because he wept and lamented over the sins of the people.
Others, one of the prophets — Elisha, for the excellence of his wisdom.
And so the error held about Jesus was divided among them.
So even today men are divided, seeking salvation, seeking blessedness;
for each one sets his blessedness in the thing he loves, and this he worships in place of God —
for, as Augustine says, that is worshipped by a man which he loves above all the rest.
Then He demands of His disciples their own confession of Him:
You, set apart from the others, who are not of the crowd, but My disciples —
to you it is given to know the mystery of the kingdom of God,
you who are the light of the world and the salt of the earth —
whom do you say that I am?
If others err with a false opinion of Me, you ought not to err,
who have been so long with Me, and heard My teaching, and seen such great miracles.
Then Peter answered for them all — for himself and for the others,
for all knew one thing and felt the same;
yet he, as the elder, the prince and head of the others, became the mouth of the whole company,
and before all and for all he answered, who was to preside over all and profit all.
Peter, taught not by flesh and blood, but by the Father in heaven,
seeing Him both Son of God and man, embraced both,
and confessed the truth of the divine and human nature in one same person:
You are the Christ — that is, the man anointed with the oil of grace above your fellows;
and the Son of God — the natural Son, not adopted as others are, of one same nature with God the Father;
and he adds: of the living God — the unchangeable and eternal,
to set Him apart from the false gods, who do not live.
prayer: whom blessed Peter confessed to be the true Son of God,
You too do I, unworthy, confess with my whole heart and mouth,
that You are the Christ, the Son of the living and eternal God;
and I beg You, most merciful Lord,
grant me to confess You in deed,
and by my works, denying You not, to be found faithful,
that I may obtain Your blessing with Peter,
and stand firm in faith and in work together with him.
Grant me also, my King and my God,
that, following You in conformity of will, I may fulfil Your good pleasure in all things,
and wholly abstain from all offense against You;
and so, by Your bounty, persevere unto the end. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Sic doctor Evangelicus debet primo errores destruere, ut melius possit veritatem adstruere.
latinJewel.english: So must the teacher of the Gospel first destroy errors, that he may the better build up the truth.
